reviews.review_description:
This game has been a great one through all but there is some things that could be better. 
First the game is kinda short but still good
Second is that there is AI in the cut scenes which I don’t mind but based off other reviews they don’t seem to like it but I do though I think they could add some more depth into the cut scenes and in characters. That’s all I wanted to say and hope the story gets more depth and gets to be longer for some long vr plays

When I started I was like " Woah this game looks cool!" Little did I know it looks like AI slop and every time there's a comic scene, I fall through the map and land on a roof of a building. This game is so BORING. I have no IDEA what to do. I was in an alleyway trying to figure out where to go bc there are SO MANY DEAD ENDS. Also why are my hands the size of a toddlers hands? I'm sure the main character you're playing as is a grown man. Anyways this game is buns idk why people are glazing it literally been the same since 22

developer_response.body: Thanks for feedback. Im working on an update to adress the toddler hands and few other things ;) Make sure to join community and  'll help you out exploring the story without getting lost 👣

reviews.review_description: Played through the Demo of this and the campaign does Look amazing, Love the story, But Please improve the voice acting a Little bit especially during Combat because it sounds Like it was made by AI since they say the same thing and they sound completely Emotionless at times so please Add more Expressions and More Emotion to the enemy voices in the game to add to the Immersion, But i'll admit I did get lost during the alley scene and the Quantum computer scenes so maybe add Subtle hints Like Graffiti (In alley level) and Like signs and stuff (Fke the quantum computer level) but apart from that keep up the good Work and I can't wait to see what the final project Looks like! 👍🏻

Immediate one star for use of AI "art" and voices. I hope the story isn't AI and it doesn't really feel like it, it has more a "Hey, wouldn't it be cool if.." vibe. Still wouldn't be surprised if they had some.. ideas from ChatGPT (which would pretty much just be cyberpunk fanfic from reddit anyway)

 The gameplay, was fun, I'll admit. Jank and wouldn't recommend it to anyone but if you ever played like Conan Exiles or an equally jank game, maybe you'd get a kick out of it. Got softlocked only once, had to quit to main menu and it fixed it. Skipped every cutscene once I got bored of AI slop images. Sandbox is lame and only did one assassin mission before I got bored. (Beat my time of 7.8 seconds on The Office! You can't >:) 
 Btw it looks like game stopped development around 2024.. sad.. somehow

reviews.review_title: One of the greatest games on vr
reviews.review_description: THIS GAME IS VERY GOATED BUT THERE NEED TO BE JS A FEW FIXES PLEASE: 1: js add more gore to it and make us able to customize how many guards and also js make the sandbox more fun and it is very fun but it js needs a lil more stuff

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! There’s a secret door behind you in Sandbox City that teleports you to the Sandbox and Arcade Arena, where you can customize the number of guards, choose enemy types and swarms, and also use cheats from the pause menu to spawn more weapons.

reviews.review_description:
It’s a simple shoot em up game with bullet time mechanics. Yeah it’s crude, a work in progress but you know what…it work! Not all games have to be triple a titles. For what it is, it’s fun! Especially sandbox mode! 

I hope the dev keeps going with it it’s got potential. 

Dev- Don’t do like some devs do and throw every idea they have into it. Just keep it stupid simple. (Kiss method)

The only small Croatian I have is the baby hands, you need to do something about that. Then again…it’s hilarious to see!

This game is actually pretty fun. The missions are fun, makes you feel like a real spy. The story is pretty engaging and well written. The sandbox was a little confusing for me. I had no idea where to go tbh. But that could've just been me lol. 

Regardless, this game is great. I like the characters and lore of the story. The physics are pretty good too. Could be tweaked a bit but they are manageable. The graphics are also good. 

One thing however, the AI. Upon opening this game, you'll realize there's a LOT of use of AI. The opening menu, cutscenes, and dialogue are all displayed with AI generated images. While using AI isn't horrible for a game, it kinda shows a lack of effort tbh. 

It's still a great game and I enjoy it. It's just the AI generated content that makes me feel a lack of effort into the actual design of the images used for the story. Still a well made game. Good work.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the comprehensive feedback! Much appreciated!

There’s definitely lots of room for improvement, and we hope to refine the experience further going forward.
The development is currently a solo effort and unfortunately compromises had to be made to realize things with the tools & skill at hand.

Look forward to new patches in the future to improve gameplay, stability & content.

reviews.review_description: I had hopes for this but after seeing no movement from the devs on, update fixes patches, more to the game I’d strongly advise people not waste their money, concept was great gameplay was cool, it’s basically stride fates but with less to it, if the company pulled their finger out I’d have happily showcased this on my channel (small but still a channel with over 100 people) it’s sad to see something with a good idea falling before it’s started.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! This games development is currently a solo effort and development but was paused because of burnout. Recently things have kicked off again, so expect updates to  gameplay, stability & content!
We’d love to hear more feedback on our discord!

reviews.review_title: Hard bullet if it was vibe coded
reviews.review_description:
No doubt in my mind this game was vibe coded, the assets look like something chatgpt would give you if you asked it to make a dystopian technologically advanced world

The slow motion isnt slow and is used up way to quick, enemies have kevlar for skin but their limbs also randomly pop off when shot, and the height and hand size is off i feel like a toddler

Also the ai is stupid and will break if you hold an enemy in front of you

Also why have toggleable slow motion if the game slows down whenever an enemy throws a punch or shoots? I couldnt even get past the story before i gave up on this lazy shovelware of a game
There was a lot of Ai and it would glitch and when it worked the guns did not work I need my money back

So this was after I uninstalled it :( because everytime i try to adjust the height and even when I updated my height to the tallest and restarted my vr it still had me short like very short and it tons of bugs but the game itself is i liked it
Loved the game just wish it was more guns so i can feel like john wick but so far this game is fun and possible will have a great future hopefully

developer_response.body: Big combat gore update coming today!

reviews.review_title: Really bad, or maybe just not for me
reviews.review_description:
This feels like a demo.  No seated mode (the game tells you you can just adjust height in the menu, but this doesn't really work regardless).  As a result, you have baby hands.  I ran around pinching dudes with my tiny baby hands, which was fun and silly for a bit, but upon getting a gun, I was disappointed that the gun mechanics feel VERY arcadey and toy-like.  

It just wasn't fun.  

Kudos to the dev for always offering steep discounts on the game, and even now, it's free, but I won't play it again.

reviews.review_title: Needs a lot of work, has potential
reviews.review_description: The cutaway scenes are like reading a comic book.  World be better if it was acted out with the characters.  Needs more than one way to play the game.  It now has point A to point B to point C.  Would be better if there was more than a straight line from A to C to play.  More choices to make that are not all dead ends and with more bad guys spawning.  The mechanics have a few glitches. The grid/grill doors that automatically open up/down sometimes close when you don't want them to.  The regular doors also will close right in a middle of a fight causing you to be stuck inside elevator or behind door for a few seconds while bad guys fast approach.  The VO said the chip would "show me" where the controls for the camera was but did not. I stumbled upon it by accident.  Needs to not be the exact same every time you play it. Randomizers that add more bad guys, place them in different locations, etc.  Too easy to memorize the path of least resistance. When will you finish the storyline?
Just so many bugs and hands r tiny HORRIBLE!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback.
We're working to improve the experience in upcoming patches!

reviews.review_description: one of the junkiest games i have ever played on vr but it was free to play so i can't knock it too hard
The absolute worst kind of slop. I hope I didn't pay money for this.
Very obvious ai images used throughout the game.
didn't play for 10 minutes it keeps kicking me out of the game
Okay physics and it feels empty

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! Look forward to new patches to improve the experience in the future.

And Happy New Year!

developer_response.body: Thanks, which mode did you play? Answer this in the community

reviews.review_description:
Updated Review (original below)
Checked out this game again after a few months to see if improvements had been implemented and pleased to see that not only has the combat been worked on, but also the crouch function had been added.
Although there are still quite a lot of rough edges, the ideas and the implementation of the developers vision is very much intact, I've upped my review to 4 stars, based mostly on the fun I had during my 1 hour playtime (which I will post on my channel at the end of May).
I like the comic book cut scene (story explanation) style, but it does tend to stretch out of view when recording, if the images where fixed to the headset view it would look much better, the collision detection is still a bit clunky, but I'm sure this small team will constantly work on improving that.
The hacking system is very very nice and excellently implemented and the thing I liked the most was that the game does not hold your hand at all, as you have to navigate your way around the environments by listening to the audio prompts very carefully.
There is a great game here, but it does still need a lot of polish.

Original Review
Tried it out earlier and for 5.99 it's worth it (just to try), but as a 63-year old man with mobility problems the complete lack of an auto-crouch option in the settings makes the game mostly unplayable, game needs a lot of work, although there are good ideas here and a couple of very nice mechanics (the hacking for example, very nicely implemented), it has issues such as quite often when you come out of the slow-down mechanic, you are unable to make contact with the enemies, although they can continue to deal damage to you (the player character), collision detection (most notably on ladders) is comedically poor... I appreciate the game is in early access and it's being constantly updated, but like my title says, it does still need lots of work, but please all VR Devs need to stop excluding the physically disabled.

This is as early access as it gets- don’t expect much content. Only buy if you want to support the devs to complete their vision.

Suggestions:
-most games have you hold both trigger and grip to make a fist. The choice to only make fist with grip is questionable. This means making a fist IRL causes character to extend pointer fingers, and extending pointer fingers IRl causes him to make fists
-from what little story is available, can already tell waypoints are a must. Struggled to find my way around
-more weapons, and a way to stow melee weapons

reviews.review_title: The Best Melee shooter on Quest!
reviews.review_description:
Let me tell you this is a one of kind VR game! You step into shoes of Declan, a broken man. An edge operator. The combat bullet-time, kicking, hacking and character backstories in the story mode have a real feel to them

The story about Cortex killing your girlfriend for leaking the brain chip tech feels like a dystopian story we are heading for... Or maybe it's just me?

My favvourite part is defo acting like a villain, hacking is also pretty neat, switching between guns, hands, feet and even pens lol..
This game is way more cinematic than I had initially thought! I recommend it for anyone seeking a single-player action-packed thrill ride!

developer_response.body: It is crazy, yes. But make sure to check the LATEST update now!!

reviews.review_description: Love the idea of this game but it's still very rough and a bit clumsy to work with. You can see what it's meant to be and if the devs are able to make it look better, play smoother, fix some interactions etc, it could be a really satisfying game.

The graphics are striking and sharp, the vocal acting is superb and mixed very well. The atmosphere is enticing and draws me in. The combat is exhilarating but not exhausting. I'm compelled to learn more about not only the character but the entire world. Thankful and hopeful the team can put it all together because this would be a welcomed addition to any VR player's library. The development team is hard at work making the appropriate adjustments to make the experience more accessible and engaging.
This is a fun arcade style game. You can vary your combat in intense situations. The immersion is great and it draws you in for more action. A worthy addition to any library

developer_response.body: Thanks! Watch out what came in latest update

reviews.review_description: Everything is great from the story to the voice acting to the guns everything it's just the hands need a little bit more work on because whenever you adjust your size the hands go bigger or smaller not just the controller length and height
Good Game good graphics
The trailer showcases a lot of crazy moves, a user tutorial to start players off is recommended to maximize user experience

“Deceptive” perfectly describes this game. I went in expecting another “time moves as fast as you do” type of experience, but it turned out to be so much more. You’re thrown into the shoes of a protagonist who channels Neo from The Matrix and John Wick. Keanu vibes are everywhere and that’s a good thing because behind the Gun-Fu action there is a compelling story. In the game, you can choose how to take down enemies and can be as active as you want. Take them out from afar with your gun but I always prefer an aggressive approach. As I take down the baddies, I am ducking, dodging and throwing combo punches until my heart is pounding out of my chest. My favorite moment came when an enemy tried to deliver a roundhouse kick, I slowed time, ducked, and delivered an uppercut straight to his nards. In addition to your fists, you can use guns, knives, and even items around you to take out enemies, all of which they react to. And once you finish the story, there are additional modes to keep the action going.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the detailed and thoughtful review! It’s amazing to hear how much you’re enjoying the Gun-Fu action and the freedom to approach combat your way. The comparisons are an honor, and we’re thrilled you had such an immersive experience with the slow-motion action and combo moves.

Your feedback about the story and additional modes is incredibly valuable – we’re committed to keeping the action fresh and exciting. Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts, and we can’t wait to bring you even more in future updates!

reviews.review_description:
Decent start and reminded me of Max Payne with the cut scenes.  Having to crouch is a bit of a pain (especially if you have old knees) so mapping a button would be better for me.  Also prefer point to move rather than with sticks as I am not great with movement.

Fun game to try, but not sure it's for me

Will get better I suppose with update.
Need an option to define shoulders wideness : I feel I'm stuck in a narrow body.

Looked forward to this game but it is not optimized, stutters and needs lots of polishing.

Id say it is in an early alpha
I started the game and was met with ugly uninspired environments and textures, asset flips? Also there are ai images everywhere, do better please. Instant turn off.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We’re sorry the initial experience didn’t meet your expectations. I hope you play further and discover the wonderful neon lit alleyways and openworld sandbox. The art is actually made by several artists via different processes. We’re taking your input seriously as we work on updates. If you have any further suggestions, we’d love to hear them – your feedback helps us grow!

reviews.review_description: This has all the great groundwork for an awesome game! Looking forward to seeing  what they come up with !

The physics in this game are incredible, I've talked personally to the dev and he's a great dude. The hand to hand combat is some of the best I've played in an app lab game! I recommend playing this game because there's so much potential in the future. I'm happy to be one of the first people to really get into game.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your enthusiastic review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the physics and hand-to-hand combat in the game. Your support and kind words about the dev mean a lot to us. We're committed to unlocking even more potential in future updates, and we're grateful to have dedicated players like you from the start. Keep enjoying the game, and stay tuned for more exciting features!

reviews.review_title: One of the worst VR purchases
reviews.review_description:
I buy all sorts of VR games from App Lab to EA steam. I’m not the most picky VR player but this one is just bad.

It’s everything I want in a VR game too, Max Payne meets Cyberpunk. But wow the fighting is awful. Like 2015 fighting quality. 

You have to bend way down to go down a tunnel. 

After the long tutorial level it crashed and ruined my whole save file. 

The graphics are very bad too. Like a unity asset flip simulator. But on VR I’m forgiving on bad graphics.

developer_response.body: Hey thanks for the review. Sorry you had some issues. Just to let you know it is not recommendable to play in your bed! Good that you are forgiving on graphics in VR, but we use the best grahpics engine Unreal. If you are serious on giving feedback, join the community it’s a warm little place of good folks :) next update will have massive upgrades and patches to the beast of the game already. Hope to still have you on board for that!

reviews.review_description: The story is great but the only problem is it need more work bc its quite early and i will come back to it in a couple of months to see how well it has improved

I’m a big fan of the sort of cyberpunk aesthetic and themes in general and being able to experience it in VR is really amazing. As of writing this it’s still in early access 2.0 but from what’s available it’s very good, the fighting is great I enjoy the fact that bullet time isn’t manually triggered and has a requirement that isn’t just a timer, I think punching is fairly satisfying but it could get touched up slightly, and dodging in real life as opposed to having a dodge button is also very cool. The hacking is fairly intuitive as well and it’s pretty fun honestly sort of like unlocking a safe, now onto other matters.

The story is presented in a very unique way for a VR game in the visual form of comic style panels that aside from the text are also voiced over, aside from that you’ll get dialogue and exposition as you play through the game in key sequences and even some optional encounter dialogue stuff which surprised me as I wasn’t expecting an optional encounters. As far as I’m aware from some videos and Twitter posts AI was used but as opposed to the sometimes popular belief that it’s a negative I think it actually enhances the experience and allows the devs to put more time into other things.

The areas you go to and the sights you see are also spectacular even for a quest game. There’s also some lore and more to come and it’s honestly impressive what the devs were able to whip up here. This game radiates a lot of passion and dedication if I have to say it. It’s a bit janky at times but it’s more fun and engaging than it is buggy. If this game continues to be worked on which it will and is still being worked on. It has a possibility to be a best seller in my opinion and I’m very excited for future updates and the full release at which point I will be updating my review

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review 🥷🌆 we are excited that you love the cyberpunk lore as well 🦾

reviews.review_description: It’s a good game but I can’t get pass the first part because I have stick drift on one of the controllers

The game is def going to give you a workout 🏋️ you will feel the burn fighting 20 enemies non stop. The story is well writing and I felt the story kinda some parts I could relate to.I’m not done with the story yet but it’s on my to do list fersher ima be streaming and making videos on tik tok and twitch check me out @PANDABZ_OFFICAL and big shoutout to the dev for caring for community

reviews.review_description: would give 5 stars but i noticed a glitch where if you jump you take fall damage and if you sit during loading screens or cutscenes your player model turns smaller

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We're glad you enjoyed the game, but we apologize for the glitches you encountered. We'll look into the issues with fall damage and the player model size during loading screens and cutscenes. Your input helps us improve the game, and we appreciate your understanding. Stay tuned for updates, and we hope you'll continue to enjoy playing!
